_meta.status: DECLARED — implementation lands in Phase C1 of the 2.0 federation work package.
_meta.stability: Frozen for the loom-generation lifetime per ADR-002. Shape additions that preserve wire compatibility (new optional response fields) are allowed; breaking changes require a new generation.
_meta.relation_to_classic: Same request shape (bytes-identical). Response shape differs: loom wraps ScanIngestResult in a BatchResponse-style envelope — new_finding_ids becomes succeeded (BatchResponse.succeeded), per-finding ingest failures become failed (BatchResponse.failed, empty in 2.0 until per-finding failure tracking lands), counts move to a stats sibling, warnings stay at the top level.

request_optional_fields.scanned_paths: list[str]  # OPTIONAL. The authoritative set of files the scanner visited this run, including clean files with zero findings. Wire-compatible addition (omitting it preserves prior behaviour). When mark_unseen is true, the absent-fingerprint sweep is driven off {files-with-findings} ∪ {scanned_paths}, so a file whose last/only finding was fixed (and is therefore absent from findings) is still reconciled to unseen_in_latest and its linked issue cascade-closes. With scanned_paths non-empty, mark_unseen=true + findings=[] is a valid fully-clean scan (no longer a 400). Unknown clean paths (no prior file record) are skipped, never created.

response_shape.succeeded: list[str]  # server-generated finding ids for newly-created findings (was: new_finding_ids in classic)
response_shape.failed: list[BatchFailure]  # per-finding ingest failures; always present as [] today. Populated when future per-finding failure tracking lands (non-breaking addition).
response_shape.warnings: list[str]  # validation warnings from db._validate_scan_findings (e.g. unknown severity coerced to 'info')

shape_decl.external_adapter_contract: Filigree does not parse SARIF on this endpoint. External SARIF adapters MUST map SARIF result.partialFingerprints or result.fingerprints into each scan-results finding.fingerprint before POSTing. Filigree then preserves finding.fingerprint through ingest, readback, promotion, fingerprint dedup, stale/fixed transitions, and reopen-on-regress lifecycle handling. Fingerprint-less legacy findings remain accepted and use the file/source/rule/line heuristic.
shape_decl.rationale: BatchResponse-style wrapper per ADR-002 §1 and work-package Phase A2. Scan-results has mixed semantics (batch ingest + summary counts); the envelope honours the batch-semantics-where-applicable rule by exposing succeeded/failed (per-finding granularity) while preserving the counts signal in a stats sibling. Named subtype so future loom endpoints with similar mixed semantics have a pattern to follow.

examples.name: success_empty_findings_clean_scan
examples.note: Clean scan: no findings submitted, succeeded + failed both empty, all stats counters zero.

examples.name: success_one_finding_with_clarion_metadata
examples.note: Representative federation-emitter case. succeeded contains the server-generated finding id (was: new_finding_ids in classic); stats mirrors ScanIngestResult counts minus new_finding_ids (now surfaced as succeeded) and warnings (now top-level).

findings.message: Use of eval() on untrusted input
findings.suggestion: Replace eval() with ast.literal_eval() or a parser for the expected grammar.

examples.name: success_wardline_sarif_adapter_fingerprint
examples.note: Representative Wardline SARIF-adapter case. Filigree does not parse SARIF here; the external adapter maps SARIF partialFingerprints/fingerprints to finding.fingerprint before POSTing so fingerprint identity survives Filigree ingestion and later lifecycle operations.

examples.name: success_clean_file_via_scanned_paths
examples.note: Close-on-fixed driver. An agent fixed the only finding in src/a.py; the re-scan carries no findings for it but lists it in scanned_paths with mark_unseen=true. Filigree flips the now-absent finding to unseen_in_latest and cascade-closes its linked issue. Cascade outcomes are logged, not surfaced in the envelope.

examples.name: error_missing_findings
examples.note: Loom uses the same flat error envelope as classic (ErrorResponse TypedDict: error + code + NotRequired details). ErrorCode enum value VALIDATION.

body.error: findings is required (use [] for a clean scan)
